entry.title: Πολιτική θύελλα για τις υποκλοπές – Γιατί παραμένουν στο αρχείο
entry.summary: Supreme Court Prosecutor Konstantinos Tzavellas ruled on 27 April 2026 that no new evidence justifies reopening the dormant Predator wiretap file. The decision triggered a major political storm, with PASOK and other opposition parties accusing the government and the judiciary of a cover-up.

entry.title: Επίθεση 89χρονου ενόπλου σε ΕΦΚΑ Κεραμεικού και Εφετείο – Πέντε τραυματίες
entry.summary: Five people were injured on Tuesday morning when an 89-year-old scrap collector opened fire first at an EFKA office in Kerameikos and then at the Athens Court of Appeal in Loukareos. He took a taxi between locations; police recovered his shotgun and evacuated the courthouse. None of the victims are in critical condition.

entry.title: Αναβάθμιση Χ.Α. και από τον STOXX
entry.summary: European index provider STOXX is upgrading the Athens Stock Exchange to developed-market status from 21 September — the final piece in Greece's 13-year journey back to the elite tier alongside earlier moves by S&P DJI, FTSE and MSCI. JPMorgan estimates around $1.1bn in passive inflows to Greek equities.

entry.title: Νίκος Ανδρουλάκης για υπόθεση υποκλοπών: Καταθέτουμε αίτημα για εξεταστική επιτροπή
entry.summary: In an emergency news conference, PASOK leader Androulakis announced his party will file a motion to set up a parliamentary inquiry committee on the Predator wiretaps, accusing the Supreme Court of "insulting and undermining" the justice system and pointing to alleged backroom deals between Maximos Mansion and a former officer.

entry.title: Ισραήλ: Εντολή μαζικής εκκένωσης πόλεων στον νότιο Λίβανο
entry.summary: Israel's IDF has ordered the mass evacuation of more than 12 towns and villages in southern Lebanon, telling residents to move further north. The order is part of an escalation against Hezbollah despite a fragile ceasefire, deepening Lebanon's humanitarian crisis with hundreds of thousands already displaced.

entry.title: Ηλικιωμένος άνοιξε πυρ στον ΕΦΚΑ Κεραμεικού
entry.summary: An 89-year-old scrap collector burst into the EFKA social-security office in Kerameikos with a hunting rifle hidden under his coat, made his way to the fourth floor and shot a clerk in the leg. He had no known dispute with EFKA, then proceeded to the Loukareos Court of Appeal, where he shot four more people.

entry.title: Γάζα: Το νερό «όπλο» του Ισραήλ εναντίον του πληθυσμού, καταγγέλλουν οι Γιατροί Χωρίς Σύνορα
entry.summary: Médecins Sans Frontières has released a report titled "Water as a Weapon" accusing Israel of deliberately depriving Gaza's population of water as part of a campaign of collective punishment and an integral element of what MSF calls genocide. The Israeli government angrily rejects the accusations.

entry.title: Λιβύη: Τεχνικές επιτροπές για την ΑΟΖ
entry.summary: Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis met Libya's GNU PM Dbeibah and acting FM Al Baour in Tripoli; both sides agreed to advance the technical committees on EEZ and continental-shelf delimitation. Athens is keeping the channel open with Tripoli without expecting Libya to abandon the Turkey-Libya MoU, while continuing energy plans south of Crete.

entry.title: Ιράν: Εκατομμύρια νεόπτωχοι εξαιτίας του πολέμου
entry.summary: Up to 4.1 million Iranians may be pushed into poverty by the war with the US and Israel, according to the UNDP. Over 23,000 factories and businesses have been hit, costing roughly one million jobs. Iran's economy was already in crisis: per-capita income fell from about $8,000 in 2012 to $5,000 in 2024.

entry.title: Δίκη για Τέμπη: Σήμερα συνεχίζεται η διαδικασία με τις τοποθετήσεις των συνηγόρων
entry.summary: The trial over the deadly Tempi rail crash continues at the Gaiopolis venue, with defence lawyers contesting how the prosecution and civil parties are joining the case. The Greek state filed a moral-damages claim against the stationmaster and three colleagues, and after heated exchanges the court adjourned to Wednesday.

entry.title: Η επίσκεψη Μακρόν ενοχλεί την Αγκυρα
entry.summary: Pro-government Turkish media frame Macron's Athens visit and pledges of French support for Greek sovereignty as an "anti-Turkish front". Ankara is publicly muted because Macron avoided naming Turkey, even as the renewed Greek-French mutual-defence agreement provokes editorials warning of Paris's growing role in the Aegean and East Med.

entry.title: Ξεκινά η «μάχη» για τον νέο ευρωπαϊκό προϋπολογισμό
entry.summary: The European Parliament votes today in Strasbourg on its negotiating mandate for the EU's next seven-year budget (2028-2034). Rapporteurs Tavares (S&D) and Mureșan (EPP) propose a 10% bump on the Commission's draft, taking it past €2 trillion, alongside excluding pandemic debt repayments and creating new own resources like a digital tax on big tech.

entry.title: To Ιράν δηλώνει έτοιμο να μοιραστεί αμυντικές δυνατότητες με εταίρους στην Ασία
entry.summary: Iranian Deputy Defence Minister Reza Talaei-Nik told a Shanghai Cooperation Organisation meeting in Bishkek that "the US can no longer dictate policy to independent states", adding Tehran is ready to share its defence capabilities with SCO members. The rhetoric escalates as Washington considers a new Iranian proposal to end the Middle East war.

entry.title: Οι ΗΠΑ σε ρόλο ρυθμιστή της αγοράς πετρελαίου
entry.summary: The US has stepped in as the swing producer of the global oil market during the Iran-war crisis, boosting exports, selectively easing sanctions and tapping strategic reserves. With the Strait of Hormuz blocking 13% of world reserves, OPEC has been left largely powerless and Washington is cementing its role as the dominant energy superpower.

entry.title: Ο Ανωγειανός που έβοσκε στη Νίσυρο και οι εικονικές σφαγές
entry.summary: Two producers on whose behalf MPs' aides allegedly intervened in the OPEKEPE subsidies case are now defendants in fraudulent-aid prosecutions. The European Public Prosecutor has asked the Greek Financial Police to probe whether political actors lobbied for farmers with strong criminal indications, including an Anogeia stockbreeder who declared grazing land in faraway Nisyros.

entry.title: Ν.Δ.: Ανοιχτή επιστολή πέντε βουλευτών με κριτική στο επιτελικό κράτος
entry.summary: Five New Democracy MPs published an open letter in Ta Nea criticising the centralised executive-state model and calling for a new governance model. They demand a stronger parliamentary role, stressing Greece does not need a state where "few decide for everyone" without accountability.

entry.title: Η Τουρκία δεν μπορεί να ελέγξει τις εξελίξεις
entry.summary: Opinion piece arguing Turkey is losing control of unfolding geopolitical events and is therefore ramping up rhetoric and actions against Greece — more frequent airspace violations, harassment of a Dutch research vessel off Kasos, and a sharpening role for FM Hakan Fidan, increasingly seen as a possible Erdogan successor.

entry.title: Ο πόλεμος «φρενάρει» τον παγκόσμιο τουρισμό
entry.summary: Almost two months into the US-Israel war on Iran, the Middle East conflict is hammering global tourism: jet-fuel-driven airfare hikes and flight cancellations are pricing many travellers out of summer trips, with "staycations" becoming the year's standout trend. Even Greece's prices are running well above last summer.

entry.title: CNN: Οι ΗΠΑ επιταχύνουν τις απελάσεις ανήλικων μεταναστών
entry.summary: CNN reports that the US administration is speeding up deportation procedures for unaccompanied migrant children in custody by weeks or months. Children as young as four are being summoned to repeated court appearances — sometimes without legal counsel — with officials citing trafficking and cartel-exploitation risks.

entry.title: Ακρίβεια και αύξηση πωλήσεων γέμισαν τα κρατικά ταμεία το 2025
entry.summary: Greek tax revenue hit €426.95 billion in 2025, up €26.87 billion (+6.7%) year-on-year, AADE data shows. Growth was driven by both higher sales and inflationary nominal-value pressures. 79 of 87 economic sectors saw increases, with building construction posting the largest jump.
